Version
  • API Reference
  • API Reference
GitHub

API Reference

Sequence & Protein

BioToolkit.dna_molecular_weight — Method
dna_molecular_weight(sequence; stranded=:single)

Estimate the molecular weight of a DNA or RNA sequence in Daltons. stranded=:double returns the weight of both strands combined. Ambiguous IUPAC bases are averaged across their canonical possibilities.

source
BioToolkit.dotmatrix — Method
dotmatrix(seq1, seq2)

Compute a dot-plot matrix comparing two sequences. Returns a Matrix{Int8} where 1 = match, 0 = mismatch. Rows correspond to seq1, columns to seq2.

source
BioToolkit.gc_skew — Method
gc_skew(sequence)

Compute cumulative GC skew values. At each position, +1 for G, -1 for C. Returns a Vector{Int} of length equal to the sequence.

source
BioToolkit.melting_temp — Method
melting_temp(sequence; rna=false, method=:wallace)

Estimate oligonucleotide melting temperature in degrees Celsius.

The default :wallace method uses the classic 2/4 rule: 2 * (A + T/U) + 4 * (G + C). rna=true treats U as the thymine equivalent, but DNA sequences with U are also accepted and counted as thymine-like bases.

method=:basic uses the longer-oligo approximation 64.9 + 41 * (G + C - 16.4) / N where N is the number of canonical bases.

source
BioToolkit.minimum_skew — Method
minimum_skew(sequence)

Find genome positions where the GC skew is minimum (potential replication origins). Returns a Vector{Int} of 1-based positions.

source
BioToolkit.aliphatic_index — Method
aliphatic_index(sequence)

Calculate the aliphatic index: the relative volume occupied by aliphatic side chains.

source
BioToolkit.extinction_coefficient — Method
extinction_coefficient(sequence)

Calculate the molar extinction coefficient at 280 nm (M⁻¹ cm⁻¹). Uses the Pace formula: ε = 1490×nY + 5500×nW + 125×nC

source
BioToolkit.gravy — Method
gravy(sequence)

Calculate the Grand Average of Hydropathicity (GRAVY) using Kyte-Doolittle values.

source
BioToolkit.instability_index — Method
instability_index(sequence)

Compute the Guruprasad instability index. Values < 40 suggest a stable protein in vitro.

source
BioToolkit.isoelectric_point — Method
isoelectric_point(sequence; precision=0.01)

Estimate the isoelectric point (pI) using the IPC bisection algorithm.

source
BioToolkit.protein_mass — Method
protein_mass(sequence; type="monoisotopic")

Calculate molecular weight of a protein sequence in Daltons. Adds water mass (18.01524 mono / 18.01056 avg) for the peptide bond.

source
BioToolkit.protparam — Method
protparam(sequence)

Compute comprehensive protein statistics (ProtParam-style). Returns a NamedTuple with all physico-chemical properties.

source

Alignment & MSA

BioToolkit.local_align — Method
local_align(left, right; kwargs...)

Backward-compatible alias for smith_waterman.

source
BioToolkit.needleman_wunsch — Method
needleman_wunsch(left, right; kwargs...)

Explicit wrapper for the global pairwise alignment path.

source
BioToolkit.smith_waterman — Method
smith_waterman(left, right; kwargs...)

Explicit wrapper for the local pairwise alignment path.

source

Structural Biology

Genomics & Intervals

BioToolkit.read_embl — Method
read_embl(filepath::String)

Parses an EMBL flat file into a vector of AnnotatedSeqRecord objects.

source
BioToolkit.read_swissprot — Method
read_swissprot(filepath::String)

Parses a SwissProt/UniProt .dat file into a vector of AnnotatedSeqRecord objects.

source

Phylogenetics & Population Genetics

BioToolkit.distance_matrix — Method
distance_matrix(sequences::Vector{<:AbstractString}; method=:hamming)

Computes an N x N symmetric distance matrix for a list of sequences. If method=:hamming, it leverages SIMD/SWAR byte-mismatch tracking. If method=:alignment, it computes Needleman-Wunsch identity inversion.

source
BioToolkit.felsenstein_likelihood — Method
felsenstein_likelihood(tree::PhyloTree, alignment::Dict{String, String}, model::SubstitutionModel)

Compute the log-likelihood of a phylogenetic tree given an alignment using Felsenstein's pruning algorithm.

source
BioToolkit.maximum_likelihood_tree — Method
maximum_likelihood_tree(alignment::Dict{String, String}; model::SubstitutionModel=JC69(), initial_tree::Union{Nothing, PhyloTree}=nothing)

Find the Maximum Likelihood tree for a given alignment.

source
BioToolkit.neighbor_joining — Method
neighbor_joining(D::Matrix{Float64}, names::Vector{String})

Executes the Neighbor-Joining agglomerative clustering algorithm on an NxN distance matrix. Returns a standard Newick tree string representation suitable for direct visualization in tools like Phylo.jl or Makie.jl.

source
BioToolkit.optimize_branch_lengths! — Method
optimize_branch_lengths!(tree::PhyloTree, alignment::Dict{String, String}, model::SubstitutionModel; max_iter=10)

Optimize branch lengths of a given tree to maximize likelihood using a simple coordinate ascent approach.

source
BioToolkit.Locus — Type
Locus{T}

A genetic locus storing a tuple of alleles. T is usually String or Char or Int. For a diploid individual, this will hold two alleles.

source
BioToolkit.PopGenIndividual — Type
PopGenIndividual{T}

An individual containing a unique identifier and an array of loci.

source
BioToolkit.Population — Type
Population{T}

A collection of individuals comprising a population or sub-population.

source
BioToolkit.allele_frequencies — Method
allele_frequencies(pop::Population, locus_idx::Int)

Calculates the frequency of each allele at a specific locus across the population. Returns a Dict mapping allele -> frequency.

source
BioToolkit.amova — Method
amova(distance_matrix::Matrix{Float64}, pop_sizes::Vector{Int})

Performs a basic one-way AMOVA given a pairwise square distance matrix of individuals and a vector designating how many individuals belong to each sequential subpopulation. Returns (PhiST, VarianceWithin, Variance_Among).

source
BioToolkit.ehh — Method
ehh(alignment::MultipleSequenceAlignment, core_site::Int, distance_sites::Int)

Calculates Extended Haplotype Homozygosity (EHH) at a core site extending out to a certain distance (number of sites). Requires phased haplotypes in the alignment.

source
BioToolkit.estimate_ne_ld — Method
estimate_ne_ld(pop::Population, locus_pairs::Vector{Tuple{Int, Int}})

Estimates Effective Population Size (Ne) using the Linkage Disequilibrium method (Hill 1981). $E[r^2] pprox 1/(3Ne) + 1/n$.

source
BioToolkit.estimate_ne_temporal — Method
estimate_ne_temporal(pop1::Population, pop2::Population, generations::Int, locus_idx::Int)

Estimates Effective Population Size (Ne) through temporal changes in allele frequencies (Nei & Tajima 1981 approximate variance method) over a span of discrete generations.

source
BioToolkit.ewens_watterson_test — Method
ewens_watterson_test(pop::Population, locus_idx::Int)

Performs the Ewens-Watterson neutrality test. Returns the observed homozygosity (F) and the expected homozygosity under the neutral infinite alleles model.

source
BioToolkit.f3_statistic — Method
f3_statistic(p1::Population, p2::Population, outgroup::Population, locus_idx::Int)

Calculates the f3 statistic (P3; P1, P2) which tests if population P3 is admixed from populations P1 and P2. A negative value indicates admixture.

source
BioToolkit.f4_statistic — Method
f4_statistic(p1::Population, p2::Population, p3::Population, p4::Population, locus_idx::Int)

Calculates the f4 statistic (P1, P2; P3, P4) which tests for gene flow between P1-P2 and P3-P4 branch pairs.

source
BioToolkit.f_statistics — Method
f_statistics(populations::Vector{Population}, locus_idx::Int)

Calculates Wright's F-statistics (FIS, FST, FIT) across multiple subpopulations for a specific locus. Returns a tuple (FIS, FST, FIT).

source
BioToolkit.fu_li_d — Method
fu_li_d(alignment::MultipleSequenceAlignment)

Calculates Fu and Li's D statistic (without an outgroup). Tests for background selection or population expansion.

source
BioToolkit.fu_li_f — Method
fu_li_f(alignment::MultipleSequenceAlignment)

Calculates Fu and Li's F statistic (without an outgroup).

source
BioToolkit.g_statistics — Method
g_statistics(populations::Vector{Population}, locus_idx::Int)

Calculates GST (Nei's GST) and Jost's D. Returns a tuple (GST, Jost_D)

source
BioToolkit.genetic_distance — Method
genetic_distance(pop1::Population, pop2::Population, locus_idx::Int; method=:nei)

Calculates the genetic distance between two populations at a single locus. Supported methods:

  • :nei : Nei's standard genetic distance
  • :reynolds : Reynolds' distance (coancestry distance)
  • :cavalli_sforza : Cavalli-Sforza chord distance
source
BioToolkit.genetic_relationship_matrix — Method
genetic_relationship_matrix(populations::Vector{Population})

Calculates the Genetic Relationship Matrix (GRM) using the VanRaden (2008) method. Returns a symmetric matrix of size N x N where N is the total number of individuals.

source
BioToolkit.genotype_frequencies — Method
genotype_frequencies(pop::Population, locus_idx::Int)

Calculates the frequency of observed genotypes at a specific locus. Assumes unordered alleles (e.g. A/B is the same as B/A).

source
BioToolkit.hardy_weinberg_exact — Method
hardy_weinberg_exact(pop::Population, locus_idx::Int)

Slatkin's Exact Test for Hardy-Weinberg Equilibrium. Useful for small sample sizes where Chi-Square is inaccurate.

source
BioToolkit.hardy_weinberg_test — Method
hardy_weinberg_test(pop::Population, locus_idx::Int)

Performs a standard Chi-Square test for Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium at a bi-allelic locus. Returns the p-value.

source
BioToolkit.heterozygosity_expected — Method
heterozygosity_expected(pop::Population, locus_idx::Int)

Returns the expected heterozygosity (Nei's gene diversity) at a given locus, calculated as 1 - sum(pi^2) where pi are the allele frequencies.

source
BioToolkit.heterozygosity_observed — Method
heterozygosity_observed(pop::Population, locus_idx::Int)

Returns the observed proportion of heterozygotes at a given locus.

source
BioToolkit.ihs — Method
ihs(alignment::MultipleSequenceAlignment, core_site::Int)

Calculates a simplified Integrated Haplotype Score (iHS) for a site by integrating EHH over a range of distances.

source
BioToolkit.inbreeding_coefficient — Method
inbreeding_coefficient(G::Matrix{Float64}, ind_idx::Int)

Calculates the inbreeding coefficient (F) from the GRM diagonal. F = G[i, i] - 1.

source
BioToolkit.infer_phase_em — Method
infer_phase_em(pop::Population, locus_1::Int, locus_2::Int; max_iter::Int=100, tol::Float64=1e-6)

Infers haplotype frequencies from unphased diploid genotype data using the Expectation-Maximization (EM) algorithm (Excoffier & Slatkin 1995). Returns a Dict mapping (allele1, allele2) -> frequency.

source
BioToolkit.ld_decay — Method
ld_decay(pop::Population, physical_distances::Vector{Float64}, locus_pairs::Vector{Tuple{Int, Int}})

Calculates the decay of Linkage Disequilibrium (r^2) over physical distance. Returns a tuple (distances, rsquaredvalues).

source
BioToolkit.ld_mapping — Method
ld_mapping(pop::Population, locus_indices::Vector{Int}, window_size::Int=10)

Calculates LD (r^2) between all pairs of loci within a sliding window. Returns a Dict mapping (locus1, locus2) -> r_squared.

source
BioToolkit.linear_mixed_model_scan — Method
linear_mixed_model_scan(genotypes::Matrix{Float64}, phenotypes::Vector{Float64}, G::Matrix{Float64})

Simple GWAS scan using a Linear Mixed Model approximation (Gram-Schmidt or Score test). Returns p-values for each marker.

source
BioToolkit.linkage_disequilibrium — Method
linkage_disequilibrium(pop::Population, locus_1::Int, locus_2::Int)

Calculate D, D', and r^2 between two bi-allelic loci.

source
BioToolkit.mantel_test — Method
mantel_test(dist_matrix1::Matrix{Float64}, dist_matrix2::Matrix{Float64}; permutations::Int=999)

Performs a Mantel test between two distance matrices to test for correlation. Returns (correlation, p_value).

source
BioToolkit.migration_rate — Method
migration_rate(populations::Vector{Population}, locus_idx::Int)

Estimates the number of migrants per generation (Nm) using the Fst method: Nm ≈ (1 - Fst) / (4 * Fst) for diploid organisms.

source
BioToolkit.mismatch_distribution — Method
mismatch_distribution(alignment::MultipleSequenceAlignment)

Calculates the distribution of pairwise nucleotide differences between all pairs of sequences in the alignment. Returns a vector of difference counts.

source
BioToolkit.nucleotide_diversity — Method
nucleotide_diversity(alignment::MultipleSequenceAlignment)

Calculates nucleotide diversity (π), the average number of nucleotide differences per site between two continuous sequences randomly drawn from the population.

source
BioToolkit.patterson_d — Method
patterson_d(p1::Population, p2::Population, p3::Population, outgroup::Population, locus_idx::Int)

Calculates Patterson's D statistic (ABBA-BABA test). Tests for introgression between P3 and either P1 or P2.

source
BioToolkit.population_pcoa — Method
population_pcoa(dist_matrix::Matrix{Float64})

Principal Coordinate Analysis (Classical Multidimensional Scaling). Transforms a distance matrix into an ordination plot. Returns (coordinates, eigenvalues).

source
BioToolkit.read_genepop — Method
read_genepop(filepath::String)

Parses a GenePop format file into a vector of Population objects.

source
BioToolkit.relatedness — Method
relatedness(G::Matrix{Float64}, ind1::Int, ind2::Int)

Returns the genomic relatedness coefficient between two individuals from the GRM.

source
BioToolkit.run_fastsimcoal — Method
run_fastsimcoal(par_file::String, num_sims::Int)

Wrapper to execute fastsimcoal2 for coalescent simulations.

source
BioToolkit.run_fdist — Method
run_fdist(input_file::String, num_loci::Int, num_pops::Int)

Wrapper for fdist2 to detect loci under selection.

source
BioToolkit.run_genepop — Method
run_genepop(input_file::String, options::String)

Wrapper to execute the native genepop binary if available.

source
BioToolkit.segregating_sites — Method
segregating_sites(alignment::MultipleSequenceAlignment)

Counts the number of segregating (polymorphic) sites in a multiple sequence alignment. Ignores columns where any sequence has a gap or missing data.

source
BioToolkit.simulate_coalescent — Method
simulate_coalescent(n::Int; ne::Real=10000, mu::Real=1e-8, seq_len::Int=1000)

Simple backward-in-time coalescent simulation (constant population size). Returns a tuple (tree::PhyloTree, haplotypes::Dict{String, String}).

source
BioToolkit.site_frequency_spectrum — Method
site_frequency_spectrum(alignment::MultipleSequenceAlignment; folded::Bool=true)

Calculates the Site Frequency Spectrum (SFS) for an alignment. If folded is true, the minor allele frequency spectrum is generated. Returns a frequency distribution vector.

source
BioToolkit.sweepfinder_clr — Function
sweepfinder_clr(alignment::MultipleSequenceAlignment, grid_points::Int=100)

Composite Likelihood Ratio (CLR) test for selective sweeps (Nielsen et al. 2005). Returns CLR values across the genomic grid.

source
BioToolkit.tajimas_d — Method
tajimas_d(alignment::MultipleSequenceAlignment)

Calculates Tajima's D statistic to test for neutral evolution. Negative values suggest selective sweeping or population expansion. Positive values suggest balancing selection or population subdivision.

source
BioToolkit.watterson_theta — Method
watterson_theta(alignment::MultipleSequenceAlignment)

Estimates Watterson's Theta (Θ) from the number of segregating sites.

source
BioToolkit.wright_fisher_metapopulation — Method
wright_fisher_metapopulation(N::Vector{Int}, generations::Int, M::Matrix{Float64}; mu::Float64=1e-8, s::Vector{Float64}=zeros(length(N)))

Simulates a metapopulation under the Wright-Fisher model with a migration matrix M. M[i, j] is the proportion of population i that comes from population j.

source
BioToolkit.wright_fisher_simulation — Method
wright_fisher_simulation(N::Int, generations::Int; mu::Float64=1e-8, s::Float64=0.0, p0::Float64=0.5)

Simulates the trajectory of a bi-allelic locus under drift, mutation (mu), and selection (s) in a population of size N over many generations. Returns a vector of allele frequencies over time.

source
BioToolkit.write_genepop — Method
write_genepop(populations::Vector{Population}, filepath::String; title="BioToolkit export")

Writes populations to a GenePop format file.

source
BioToolkit.xp_ehh — Method
xp_ehh(pop1_ali::MultipleSequenceAlignment, pop2_ali::MultipleSequenceAlignment, core_site::Int)

Calculates Cross-Population EHH (XP-EHH) to detect selection between two populations.

source

Omics & Systems Biology

Databases & Utilities

BioToolkit.build_index — Function
build_index(targets::Vector{<:AbstractString}, names::Vector{<:AbstractString}=String[]; k::Int=4)

Builds a k-mer index for a database of target sequences (optimized for proteins, k <= 8).

source
BioToolkit.local_search — Method
local_search(query::AbstractString, index::KmerIndex; scoring::AbstractPairwiseScoring, x_drop::Int=10, min_score::Int=20)

Performs a BLOSUM/SubstitutionMatrix-scored BLAST-like seed-and-extend local search.

source
BioToolkit.qblast — Method
qblast(program::String, database::String, query::String; options::Dict=Dict())

Placeholder for remote BLAST search. Implementation requires HTTP and EzXML dependencies.

source
BioToolkit.run_blast — Method
run_blast(program::String, query::String, database::String; options::String="", outfmt::Int=6)

Run a local BLAST+ search. Requires the blast+ suite to be installed in the system PATH. Returns a Vector of HighScoringPair.

source
BioToolkit.HMM — Type
HMM{T<:AbstractFloat}

A Hidden Markov Model operating entirely in log-space for numerical stability.

source
BioToolkit.backward — Method
backward(hmm::HMM, sequence::AbstractVector{UInt8})

Computes the backward probability (log p(X{t+1:T} | zt = i)) array. Returns an NxL matrix.

source
BioToolkit.forward — Method
forward(hmm::HMM, sequence::AbstractVector{UInt8})

Computes the total log-probability of the sequence given the model over all valid paths.

source
BioToolkit.viterbi — Method
viterbi(hmm::HMM, sequence::AbstractVector{UInt8})

Computes the most likely sequence of hidden states (Viterbi path) and its log-probability. Returns (path::Vector{Int}, log_prob::Float64).

source
BioToolkit.predict_genes_hmm — Method
predict_genes_hmm(sequence::AbstractString; p_coding=0.01)

Uses a specialized Hidden Markov Model and the Viterbi API to predict open reading frames (genes) in nucleotide DNA mathematically over the entire structure. It out-performs naive substring scanning by intrinsically penalizing nonsense gaps and filtering overlapping false positive hits statistically.

Returns an array of (start_index, end_index) genomic tuples.

source
« HomeBenchmarks »
